Comprehensive Index of Major and Minor Hellenic & Eurasian Traditions: Canons, Epics, Hymns, Fragments

This index covers the major and minor religious, poetic, and philosophical traditions of the Hellenic world and broader Eurasia, focusing on extant corpora, canonical texts, epics, hymns, liturgies, and fragments. Where possible, numeric dates, major works, and digital resources are included.
Perseus Digital Library: Massive collection of Greek, Roman, and Anatolian texts (epics, hymns, philosophy, inscriptions)
Attalus: Detailed lists of Greek and Roman sources, events, and inscriptions (323–30 BCE)2
Internet Classics Archive: Translations of Greek and Roman philosophical and literary works
Sacred-texts.com: Manichaean, Norse, Celtic, and other Eurasian texts
CELT (Corpus of Electronic Texts): Early Irish and Celtic literature
Wikipedia (Hellenistic religion, Hellenistic period): Summaries and bibliographies54
Encyclopedia of Greece and the Hellenic Tradition: Comprehensive scholarly index37
V. Notes on Key Genres and Text Types
Epics: Iliad, Odyssey, Aeneid, Táin Bó Cúailnge, Beowulf, Shahnameh
Hymns & Liturgies: Homeric Hymns, Orphic Hymns, Carmen Arvale, Yasna, Book of the Dead, Eddaic poems
Mystery Texts: Orphic Gold Tablets, Eleusinian and Dionysian liturgies, Mithraic Liturgy
Philosophical Treatises: Plato, Aristotle, Stoic and Epicurean fragments, Zoroastrian Gathas
Magical/Divinatory Texts: Greek Magical Papyri, curse tablets, oracles, astrology manuals
For a detailed, text-by-text index, consult [Attalus]2 (Greek & Roman sources), [Perseus Digital Library], and the Encyclopedia of Greece and the Hellenic Tradition37.
For a scholarly overview, see the Journal of Hellenic Studies1.
